_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Born Here, Documented Nowhere
context: An estimated 50,000 to 100,000 children born in Malaysia lack citizenship due to gaps in the Federal Constitution, statelessness of parents, or administrative failures.

cards.text: Born in Malaysian hospitals. Cannot attend school. Cannot work.
cards.sub: Stateless children have no IC, no access to public education, and no legal path forward.

cards.h: Article 14 grants citizenship by birth, but not to all births.
cards.s: Children of Malaysian mothers married to foreign fathers were excluded until the 2024 amendment stalled.
cards.d: Article 14 grants citizenship by birth, but not to all births. Children of Malaysian mothers married to foreign fathers were excluded until the 2024 amendment stalled.

cards.h: NRD rejection rates for citizenship applications: estimated over 90%.
cards.s: Appeals take 5 to 15 years. Children age out of school while waiting.
cards.d: NRD rejection rates for citizenship applications: estimated over 90%. Appeals take 5 to 15 years. Children age out of school while waiting.

cards.h: Sabah alone accounts for an estimated 40,000 stateless children.
cards.s: Legacy of the 1990s IC project and undocumented migration creates generational exclusion.
cards.d: Sabah alone accounts for an estimated 40,000 stateless children. Legacy of the 1990s IC project and undocumented migration creates generational exclusion.

cards.text: A child did not choose where to be born.
cards.sub: Punishing children for their parents' status is cruelty with paperwork.

cards.text: Like a library card that was never printed. The book exists, the reader exists, but the system says neither qualifies.
cards.sub: Administrative gaps create invisible citizens who exist physically but not legally

cards.text: No child should be stateless in the country of their birth. The Constitution can fix this. The question is whether Parliament will.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
